title: SQL: time zone does not have effect on now()
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());
CREATE TABLE events (event_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, user_id INT REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO events (event_time, user_id) VALUES (now(), 1), (now() AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 2);
SELECT u.name, e.event_time FROM users u JOIN events e ON u.id = e.user_id WHERE e.event_time > now() - INTERVAL '1 day';
key_bug_features: now() function ignores time zone settings
